title: Source Ingestion, Parsing, OCR, Provenance, and Trust · Contract Workshop
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build provenance-preserving ingestion ledger, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of provenance-preserving ingestion ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Inventory source owner, authority, location, version, time, license, access policy, checksum, parser, and lineage for every ingested document.
Parse HTML, Markdown, PDF, office, JSON, email, and plain text through format-specific admission, size, recursion, and error contracts.
Retain page, region, reading order, table, figure, coordinate, OCR confidence, and source-image links when extraction needs layout evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Source Inventory Provenance
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 1169 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit parsed content with identity, provenance, licensing, quarantine, and untrusted-data boundaries; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Source Inventory Provenance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory source owner, authority, location, version, time, license, access policy, checksum, parser, and lineage for every ingested document. For RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Source Inventory Provenance, keep Rag Source Inventory Provenance connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

steps.title: RAG and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Rag Ingestion Quarantine Trust
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 5312 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit parsed content with identity, provenance, licensing, quarantine, and untrusted-data boundaries; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Quarantine malformed, unauthorized, poisoned, executable, hidden, or policy-violating content and always treat accepted content as untrusted data.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-ingestion-quarantine-trust-14wqyc" that uses case 8372 to admit parsed content with identity, provenance, licensing, quarantine, and untrusted-data boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-ingestion-quarantine-trust-14wqyc
# Competency: rag-ingestion-quarantine-trust.
# Case 8372: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def evidence_rag_ingestion_quarantine_trust_14wqyc(source_id: str = "manual-v3", bytes_read: int = 4096, parse_errors: int = 0, licensed: bool = True, provenance_kept: bool = True, content_trusted: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_14wqyc = "14wqyc-cyqw41-8372"
    assert evidence_variant_14wqyc
    evidence_axes_14wqyc = (
        "corpus14wqyc",
        "querycyqw41",
        "tenant8372",
        "index14wqyc8372",
        "promptcyqw418372",
        "evaluation837214wqyc",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_14wqyc)) == 6
    if not source_id or bytes_read < 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "source-admission"
    if parse_errors > 0:
        return False, parse_errors, "parse-quarantine"
    if not licensed or not provenance_kept:
        return False, bytes_read, "provenance-policy"
    if content_trusted:
        return False, bytes_read, "data-is-not-instruction"
    assert source_id == "manual-v3"
    return True, bytes_read, "quarantined-source"
